name: Meetha Kaddoo (Braised Butternut Squash With
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the squash on a cutting board.
2. Using a sharp knife, cut off the stem end, then halve it (if necessary, use a mallet to force the blade of the knife into the squash). Scoop out the seeds and fibers from each half, then scrape the flesh to remove clinging fibers. Cut each half into 1 1/2 inch wedges and peel the hard skin off. Cut the meat into 1 1/2 inch pieces and set aside.
3. Measure out the spices and place them right next to the stove in separate piles.
4. Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until very hot. Add the fenugreek seeds and fry until they turn dark brown (about 10 seconds), add the asafetida, coriander, and pepper. Stir once.
5. Immediately add the squash to the skillet. Stir rapidly to distribute the spices (about 3 to 4 minutes).
6. Sprinkle salt to taste; stir.
7. Lower the heat and cook, covered, until the squash is cooked but still holding its shape (about 15 minutes).
8. Uncover and sprinkle on the mango powder. Reduce the heat to medium and turn-fry for 2 minutes.
9. Add the jaggery and cook until it melts and coats the vegetables (3-4 minutes). Serve warm, at room temperature, or cold.
